questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: The existence of flying saucers, unidentified flying objects allegedly piloted by extraterrestrial beings, has been shown to be illusory. Space researchers have demonstrated that a number of photographs showing flying saucers are either crude forgeries or misidentifications of such earthly objects as weather balloons or small private aircraft. If the photographs mentioned above are accurate, which of the following is the best argument AGAINST the conclusion that flying objects can be conclusively shown to be man-made objects?

choices.a: Not all identifiable flying objects can be conclusively shown to be man-made objects
choices.b: The fact that a number of photographs of flying saucers are fakes does not generally disprove the phenomenon
choices.c: Some of those who claim to have witnessed flying saucers have no apparent motive for lying
choices.d: Given the size and complexity of the universe, it seems unreasonable to assume that life exists only on Earth
choices.e: Researchers who are skeptical about flying saucers inevitably have their own biases and preconceptions to their work

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: After every Student Rights Coalition signed the petition for a meeting with the university trustees, Philip must be a member of the Student Rights Coalition since his signature appears on the petition. The conclusion above is based on the central flaw in the reasoning class. All Some members of the Student Rights Coalition may not support this petition to the organic dean's positions.

choices.a: Some members of the Student Rights Coalition may not support this petition to the organic dean's positions
choices.b: It is possible that Philip's signature on the petition was forged by a member of the Student Rights Coalition
choices.c: Any member of the student body is eligible to sign a petition dealing with university affairs
choices.d: Philip may have resigned from the Student Rights Coalition after signing the petition
choices.e: Some of those who signed the petition may not be members of the Student Rights Coalition

questions.subject: verbal
questions.body: Questions 4-6: Pollution control can no longer be viewed as a national problem to be addressed by individual countries on the basis of national sovereignty. As the international effects of the Chernobyl nuclear accident make clear, pollutants do not respect political boundaries; thus every state has a legitimate stake in the environmental practices and policies of its neighbors. Which of the following would be the most logical continuation of the argument above?

choices.a: So, growth of the nuclear power industry should be halted until more rigorous safety procedures have been developed
choices.b: Hence, attempts made by one nation to impose its environmental policies on another should be resisted - if necessary, by force
